examples.title: Circular Queue Implementation
examples.description: Complete implementation of circular queue with all basic operations
examples.explanation: This code implements a circular queue using array. The key feature is using modulo operator (%) for wrap-around. isFull uses (rear+1)%MAXSIZE == front to distinguish from empty state. Time complexity: O(1) for all operations. Space: O(MAXSIZE).

examples.title: Multiple Stacks using Array
examples.description: Implementation of K stacks in single array using divide-and-conquer
examples.explanation: This implements K stacks in a single array by dividing the array equally. Each stack has its own top pointer and boundaries. Overflow occurs when a stack reaches its boundary, even if other stacks have free space. Space complexity: O(N).

examples.title: Multiple Stacks using Linked Lists
examples.description: Implementation of multiple stacks using dynamic linked lists
examples.explanation: Each stack is an independent linked list. This approach eliminates overflow as long as physical memory is available. Each stack grows dynamically without affecting others. Memory overhead is O(1) per node for pointer storage.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for push, pop, and isEmpty
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is total elements stored
